steps.title: Spot the independent clause needing boundary punctuation
steps.content: “The mission ended when the shuttle landed at Kennedy Space Center in Florida” is an independent clause that cannot be preceded by only a comma.

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary rule
steps.content:
Choice A uses a period after STS-72, which properly separates the sentences.

The second sentence begins with a participial phrase “Lasting eight days and twenty-two hours,” correctly followed by a comma before the independent clause.

Choices B, C, and D all position the independent clause “the mission ended when...” after only a comma, which creates a comma splice and violates the rule requiring a period, semicolon, or comma + coordinating conjunction to separate independent clauses.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
